summary: Iteration 2 — Role separation testing. Backend: 21/21 pytest passing (added 7 new role-separation tests covering /patients/me, caregiver-only POST /patients with 403 for patient, patient-list scoping, register-as-patient auto profile+trolley creation). Frontend: caregiver and patient flows fully verified on mobile viewport (390x844). Found and FIXED 1 backend bug.

issues: Pre-existing console deprecation warnings: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow). Cosmetic only.

action_items: Optional cleanup: migrate shadow* style props to boxShadow (cosmetic warnings only).
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: server.py POST /api/patients referenced body.user_id but PatientIn model has no such field — would raise AttributeError 500 when caregiver creates a new patient. Replaced with user_id=None so caregiver-created patients are unlinked to user accounts (which matches frontend behavior — frontend doesn't send user_id).
GET /api/patients/me correctly enforces role==patient (403 for caregivers).
POST /api/patients correctly enforces role==caregiver (403 for patients) — verified.
Seed data backfills user_id on existing 'Aarav Sharma' record so demo patient login resolves to correct profile.
Patient register auto-creates patient profile + trolley_status with 6 compartments — verified end-to-end.
GET /api/patients filters by user_id for patient role (only own profile returned).
seed_data_creation: None — relied on existing seed (admin@trolley.health, patient@trolley.health, Aarav Sharma). Test-created TEST_* patients/users were cleaned up post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login submit testID is 'login-submit' (NOT 'sign-in-btn'). Add-patient screen testID 'add-patient-screen', form fields pt-name/pt-age/pt-condition/pt-lang-{code}/pt-submit. Patient switcher: tap 'patient-card' (caregiver only) → modal with 'add-new-patient-btn' and 'switch-to-{pid}' rows. Schedule FAB testID 'add-medicine-fab' renders only for caregiver. Patient sees 'Read-only · Ask your caregiver' banner. After file additions in /app/frontend/app/, Expo metro must be restarted (sudo supervisorctl restart expo) to register new routes — observed during testing.
rca_of_issue: Bug: POST /api/patients raised AttributeError because PatientIn model lacked user_id field but server tried body.user_id. Repro: as caregiver, POST /api/patients with valid payload — would 500. Fix: hardcoded user_id=None in create_patient since frontend never supplies user_id (caregivers create unlinked patient records). Verified by test_create_patient_caregiver_ok and full UI add-patient flow.
